2603.17387.title: CRE-T1 Preview Technical Report: Beyond Contrastive Learning for Reasoning-Intensive Retrieval
2603.17387.paper_summary: The central challenge of reasoning-intensive retrieval lies in identifying implicitreasoning relationships between queries and documents, rather than superficial se-mantic or lexical similarity. The contrastive learning paradigm is fundamentallya static representation consolidation technique: during training, it encodes hier-archical relevance concepts into fixed geometric structures in the vector space,and at inference time it cannot dynamically adjust relevance judgments accord-ing to the specific reasoning demands of each query. Consequently, performancedegrades noticeably when vocabulary mismatch exists between queries and doc-uments or when implicit reasoning is required to establish relevance. This pa-per proposes Thought 1 (T1), a generative retrieval model that shifts relevancemodeling from static alignment to dynamic reasoning. On the query side, T1 dy-namically generates intermediate reasoning trajectories for each query to bridgeimplicit reasoning relationships and uses <embtoken> as a semantic aggregationpoint for the reasoning output. On the document side, it employs an instruction+ text + <embtoken> encoding format to support high-throughput indexing. Tointernalize dynamic reasoning capabilities into vector representations, we adopt athree-stage training curriculum and introduce GRPO in the third stage, enablingthe model to learn optimal derivation strategies for different queries through trial-and-error reinforcement learning. On the BRIGHT benchmark, T1-4B exhibitsstrong performance under the original query setting, outperforming larger modelstrained with contrastive learning overall, and achieving performance comparableto multi-stage retrieval pipelines. The results demonstrate that replacing static rep-resentation alignment with dynamic reasoning generation can effectively improvereasoning-intensive retrieval performance.
2603.17387.paper_summary_zh: 中央挑戰在於推理密集型檢索的核心在於識別查詢與文檔之間的隱含推理關係，而非表面的語義或詞彙相似性。對比學習範式基本上是一種靜態表示整合技術：在訓練期間，它將層次相關概念編碼為向量空間中的固定幾何結構，而在推理時，它無法根據每個查詢的具體推理需求動態調整相關性判斷。因此，當查詢與文檔之間存在詞彙不匹配或需要隱含推理來建立相關性時，性能會顯著下降。本文提出了思維1（T1），一種生成檢索模型，將相關性建模從靜態對齊轉向動態推理。在查詢端，T1動態生成每個查詢的中間推理軌跡，以橋接隱含推理關係，並使用<embtoken>作為推理輸出的語義聚合點。在文檔端，它採用指令+文本+<embtoken>編碼格式以支持高吞吐量索引。為了將動態推理能力內化到向量表示中，我們採用了三階段的訓練課程，並在第三階段引入GRPO，使模型能夠通過試錯強化學習學習不同查詢的最佳推導策略。在BRIGHT基準上，T1-4B在原始查詢設置下表現優異，整體上超越了使用對比學習訓練的更大模型，並達到了可與多階段檢索管道相媲美的性能。結果顯示，將靜態表示對齊替換為動態推理生成可以有效改善推理密集型檢索的性能。
2603.17387.author: Guangzhi Wang et.al.
2603.17387.authors: Guangzhi Wang, Yinghao Jiao, Zhi Liu
